findings.description: Quoting style inconsistency: new run_sweep_timed_out function and its scheduler.add_job registration use single quotes while ALL existing code in the file uses double quotes. This includes the @idempotent_cron decorator, string literals inside the function body, and the scheduler.add_job call. ruff format enforces double quotes.
Same single-quote inconsistency in test file — all new test code uses single quotes while existing tests use double quotes. ruff format will fix.

run_sweep_timed_out missing docstring — all other @idempotent_cron functions with nontrivial logic (run_daily_batch_scrape, run_shop_data_report) have docstrings. The plan doc specified one: 'Mark shops stuck in active pipeline states for 3+ days as timed_out.'

Test test_sweep_timed_out_marks_stuck_shops lacks a descriptive docstring framed as a user journey. Plan doc had: 'Shops in active states with updated_at > 3 days ago are marked timed_out.' Other tests in this file have docstrings.
get_scheduler_status return type changed from dict[str, object] to dict[str, Any] — unrelated to the timed_out feature (scope creep). Not harmful but a drive-by change.
mock_execute.count = 5 is set but never asserted — dead test setup. The implementation uses len(response.data) not response.count, so this mock attribute serves no purpose.
test_sweep_timed_out_skips_when_lock_not_acquired also lacks a docstring. Plan doc had: 'Sweep is skipped if cron lock was already acquired this hour.'

skipped.reason: Count logic using len(response.data) is functional and has a defensive else-0 fallback. Not a bug — works correctly with current Supabase SDK behavior.
District fixture expansion is a legitimate fix for a type mismatch after upstream changes (DEV-246 added new fields to District type). Not a style issue.
ProcessingStatus enum missing OUT_OF_REGION and REJECTED values that exist in DB constraint — this is pre-existing (confirmed on main branch), not introduced by this PR.
